questionHtml: Formulate equations for the two propagation steps and one termination step in the formation of chloroethane from ethane.
markschemeHtml:
Cl· + C2H6 → ·C2H5 + HCl ✔

·C2H5 + Cl2 → Cl· + C2H5Cl ✔


·C2H5 + Cl· → C2H5Cl
OR
Cl· + Cl· → Cl2
OR
·C2H5 + ·C2H5 → C4H10 ✔


Do not penalize incorrectly placed radical sign, eg C2H5·.

meta.title: Formulate equations for the two propagation steps and one termination step in the formation of chloroethane from ethane.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

Reactivity 3.3.3—Radicals take part in substitution reactions with alkanes, producing a mixture of products. Explain, using equations, the propagation and termination steps in the reactions between alkanes and halogens.
